requirement: Provide secure voice communications between HQ and deployed forces

reasons.CO: Communications Services directly covers voice communication systems, protocols, and infrastructure for tactical and strategic networks.
reasons.CR: Core Services provide the foundational transport and switching capabilities required to carry voice traffic.
reasons.CP: Capabilities packages include the planning and deployment of voice communication systems for operational use.
reasons.IP: Information Products such as voice recordings, message logs, and call records are produced by voice communication systems.
reasons.BP: Business Processes govern the procedures and workflows for requesting and using secure voice channels.
reasons.CI: COI Services may leverage secure voice channels as part of their community information-sharing activities.
reasons.UA: User Applications provide the interfaces (softphones, terminals) that deployed personnel use for voice communications.
reasons.BR: Business Roles were evaluated but are not relevant — the taxonomy does not cover voice communication systems through roles alone.
